mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signals economic strength, increasing inflation expectations and pushing 10-year yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of PAYEMS changes on 20-day forward yield changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4%, labor market tightness intensifies wage pressures, leading to higher inflation expectations and rising 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in triggered periods (UNRATE < 4.0) the 20-day forward yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.1) or the hit rate is below 55%, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When core CPI inflation is above 2% and the fed funds rate is below 1%, real interest rates are deeply negative, forcing bond markets to demand higher yields to compensate for inflation risk over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with above-target inflation
failure_condition: If in triggered episodes the 20-day yield change is negative or zero in more than 50% of cases, or the average change is not significantly positive, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a demand-driven recovery, rising housing starts and GDP growth signal strong consumer demand, pushing 10-year yields up; the signal is conditional on being in a post-recession expansion regime.
regime_hint: economic expansion phase after a recession
failure_condition: If during periods classified as post-recession expansion the 20-day yield change is not positively correlated with rising HOUST and GDPC1 (rank IC < 0.05), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production over 1 month indicates economic weakness, leading to a flight-to-safety that drives 10-year yields lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a 1% monthly drop in INDPRO the 5-day yield change is positive or zero in more than 50% of cases, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation consistently drives 10-year yields higher over a 60-trading-day horizon as bond investors demand compensation for inflation risk.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward yield change is negatively correlated with CPI inflation over any 5-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are rising and the Fed keeps rates low, it signals strong demand with accommodative monetary policy, amplifying upward pressure on 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing recovery
failure_condition: If in triggered periods the 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (rank IC < 0.05), hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovery regime characterized by falling unemployment and rising retail sales, yields tend to rise as consumption boosts growth expectations over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market recovery with consumption growth
failure_condition: If during periods of falling UNRATE and rising RSAFS the 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1), hypothesis is rejected.
